nodes.label: AI for small manageable steps
nodes.grounding_span: It's fantastic when I break down the changes into small manageable steps

nodes.label: AI for non-expert languages
nodes.grounding_span: if it helps be do a bit of JavaScript, which I understand in principle but not expertly, that produces a good feeling

nodes.label: AI with reliable undo/back
nodes.grounding_span: I don't trust it. So my worst nightmare is... the AI ruins my code

nodes.label: AI for tedious code generation
nodes.grounding_span: If I know the code will be tedious to write e.g. a detailed SQL query, I'll ask the AI to do it

nodes.label: Productivity and efficiency
nodes.grounding_span: The AI just does it quicker and I can check it

nodes.label: Control over code quality and design
nodes.grounding_span: I really concentrate on keeping it in check

nodes.label: Learning and skill maintenance
nodes.grounding_span: I can ask it a series of questions and understand what is happening

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for small tasks
nodes.grounding_span: It's fantastic when I break down the changes into small manageable steps

nodes.label: Frustration with AI ruining code
nodes.grounding_span: the AI completely ruins my code. I hate it then

nodes.label: Positive feeling when AI helps with non-expert languages
Methodical decomposition into small steps
nodes.grounding_span: I break my work down into small steps

nodes.label: Proactive defensive saving (git save)
nodes.grounding_span: I git save each step before requesting the next change from the AI
